name: Balsamic Glazed Sweet and Sour Onions
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds Cipolline onions (or small ones measuring about 1 1/2 inches)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the onions, keeping and washing any root end you find.
2. Heat 4 tablespoons of virgin olive oil in a 12 to 14-inch sauté pan over medium-high heat until it begins to smoke.
3. Add butter and cook until the foam subsides.
4. Add the onions and sauté them until they are light golden brown on all sides, about 8 to 10 minutes.
5. Stir in sugar, balsamic vinegar, tomato sauce, and water. Bring the mixture to a boil.
6. Cook the onions uncovered until they reach an al dente texture, approximately 10 minutes. If the liquid evaporates too quickly, add more water gradually (no more than 1/4 cup at a time) to prevent overcooking. The sauce should just cling to the onions.
7. Transfer the onions to an earthenware dish and keep warm, or allow them to cool if you plan to serve them later as an appetizer.
Yield: 4 servings
